attacking_style: Attacks inside final 3km on sustained gradients (10%+), particularly on summit finishes. Prefers solo moves mid-climb over explosive accelerations, often on gravel or technical terrain where descending skills create separation.
team_tactics: Ineos deploys full climbing train for sustained tempo control before isolation. Team historically elevates him over co-leaders (Thomas 2019, Yates 2021) when form permits. Positioned aggressively early on climbs to avoid splits.
terrain_preference: Excels on long, steep gradients (10%+) and gravel climbs where technical descending compounds advantages. Struggles on punchy, explosive finishes and time trials. Heat tolerance appears superior to GC rivals.
vulnerability: Time trials remain persistent weakness (lost 2+ minutes at 2019 TDF). Fragile when isolated without teammates on sustained tempo climbs. Vulnerable to explosive attacks after forcing early pace. Back issues emerged under pressure (2021 Giro).
key_patterns: Attacks final 1-3km on sustained climbs, particularly gravel/technical sections (6+ observations)
Struggles with sustained tempo by rivals more than explosive attacks (5+ observations)
Post-2021 crash: dropped on tempo climbs, shows improving but subpar form (4 observations)
Time trial weakness forces aggressive climbing tactics to compensate (4 observations)
Fades when forcing pace from distance; strongest when attacking late (4 observations)
Team commitment visible through full climbing trains despite hierarchy questions (5 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Dauphine shows continued recovery from career-threatening 2021 crash, improving through races but still below pre-injury standards. Power returning in third-week racing.
